question_title: Will the next UN Security Council resolution renewing the UNMISS mandate be adopted with at least one abstention before April 30, 2026?
summary: The question requires BOTH that a UNMISS renewal resolution be adopted before April 30, 2026, AND that it include at least one abstention. The abstention component is near-certain (~95%) given 4 consecutive years of Russia+China abstentions with structural disagreements unchanged and worsening dynamics. However, the timing component is genuinely uncertain: in 2025 the vote was May 8 (after April 30), while in 2024 it was April 29 (just before). Under Trump's contentious approach to peacekeeping, the vote could easily slip past the April 30 deadline. The combined probability is approximately 0.50.

factors.description: Strong historical base rate: 6/7 UNMISS renewals (2019-2025) had at least one abstention. Only 2021 was unanimous.

Increasing abstention trend: abstentions rose from 1 to 3 over 2019-2025, with Russia+China both abstaining every year since 2022. Structural disagreements (US penholder approach, 'politicized' language) remain unchanged.

Worsening South Sudan situation in 2026: escalating violence, UNMISS ordered to evacuate bases, 280k displaced since December 2025. This leads to TOUGHER resolution language, making Russia/China abstentions more likely.
Trump administration's hawkish peacekeeping approach: 'go back to basics' strategy, warnings about UNMISS viability. Non-unanimous renewals for 4+ missions since 2025.

CRITICAL TIMING RISK: Question requires vote BEFORE April 30, 2026. In 2025, the vote was May 8 (after April 30). In 2024, it was April 29. Under Trump's contentious approach, there's ~45-50% chance the vote slips past April 30.
Small risk of non-renewal: US has warned about UNMISS viability and threatened to reconsider the mission's future. If US decides not to renew or blocks renewal entirely, question resolves NO.

reasoning:
The reflection passed with verification of all factors. Let me finalize my forecast.

## Summary

This question decomposes into two independent sub-questions:

**Sub-question 1: Will a UNMISS renewal resolution be adopted before April 30, 2026?**
- The mandate expires April 30, 2026. The Security Council Report lists UNMISS as an "April" item.
- In 2025 (first renewal under Trump), the vote was **May 8** — 8 days after mandate expiry
- In 2024 (under Biden), the vote was **April 29** — just barely before the deadline
- Under Trump's contentious "go back to basics" approach and harder negotiations, there's genuine uncertainty about whether the vote happens before April 30
- **Estimate: ~52%**

**Sub-question 2: If adopted, will there be at least one abstention?**
- 6 out of 7 UNMISS renewals from 2019-2025 had abstentions (only 2021 was unanimous)
- Russia and China have BOTH abstained for 4 consecutive years (2022-2025)
- Their reasons are structural: objection to "politicized" US-drafted language, climate/sexual violence references
- The trend is worsening (1→1→0→2→2→2→3 abstentions)
- The 2026 context (worse violence, Trump's tougher approach) makes even harsher language likely
- **Estimate: ~95%**

**Combined: P(YES) ≈ 0.52 × 0.95 ≈ 0.50**

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the next UN Security Council resolution renewing the UNMISS mandate adopted before April 30, 2026 is listed on the UN Security Council 2026 resolutions page at https://main.un.org/securitycouncil/en/content/resolutions-adopted-security-council-2026 and its recorded vote includes at least one abstention. Otherwise, it resolves to no.
fine_print: If no UNMISS renewal resolution is adopted before April 30, 2026, this question resolves to no. Procedural meetings without an adopted renewal resolution do not count. The question may also resolve according to other credible sources such as the UN Security Council resolutions index at https://main.un.org/securitycouncil/en/content/resolutions-0 ; UN press release coverage at https://press.un.org.
